message:
feat(cli,share): embedded-database archive support, symlink-safe reset, and partial-publish cleanup

Three safety improvements to the CLI and share pipeline:

## Embedded-database archive save/restore (cli)

When the SQLite database lives inside the storage root directory, the
archive previously double-included it (once as snapshot, once as storage
file) and restore created redundant backups. Now:
- archive_restore_db_embedded_in_storage_root() detects the embedded
  layout and branches accordingly
- archive_save excludes live SQLite and sidecars from storage payload
  via collect_files_with_exclusions
- archive_restore backs up the entire storage root as one unit when
  embedded, instead of separate DB + storage backups
- staging tempdir placed on the correct filesystem via
  archive_restore_snapshot_staging_parent()
- 3 new tests for embedded restore (existing/missing target) and save

## Symlink-safe storage reset (cli)

Extracted remove_storage_root_entry_for_reset() using symlink_metadata
to correctly identify symlinks and remove them via remove_file without
following into the target. Previously, path.is_dir() followed symlinks,
so a symlinked directory triggered remove_dir_all on the target.
Test: clear_and_reset_removes_symlinked_storage_entries_without_touching_targets

## Share publish partial-output cleanup (cli + share/bundle)

share_update_publish_generated_outputs now removes partial output before
rollback. bundle.rs tracks whether output_dir existed pre-publish; on
rollback, freshly-created-but-empty output directories are cleaned up.
finalize.rs: gate rewrite_snapshot_storage with #[cfg(test)].
Tests added for both partial failure scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
